name: QuTiP
description: The Quantum Toolbox in Python, widely used for simulating open quantum systems, control, and dynamics.
whyUseIt: QuTiP is excellent when the problem is more about quantum dynamics, noise, and state evolution than about compiling circuits to hardware.
fieldPosition: Open-system simulation, dynamics, and control analysis
engineerWorkflow: Represent Hamiltonians or density matrices, evolve the system through time, add noise channels, then analyse observables and fidelity.
managerExpects: Models that make the physical assumptions and noise choices explicit.
demoDescription: Simulate dephasing in a qubit and compare state evolution with and without environmental noise.
beginnerMistakes: Treating ideal closed-system dynamics as realistic hardware behaviour
Using the library without understanding what the Hamiltonian terms mean
